items.title: Italian prosecutors confirm journalist was hacked with Paragon spyware
items.content: Italian authorities are making progress in their investigation into a wide-ranging spyware scandal in Italy involving Paragon spyware. But the mystery of who hacked two Italia

items.title: MacBook Neo, iPhone 17e, and everything else Apple announced this week
items.content: From a new iPhone to a budget-friendly MacBook, Apple announced a series of new products this week.

items.title: Meet the MacBook Neo, Apple’s colorful answer to the Chromebook, starting at $599
items.content: Apple is offering the MacBook Neo at a more affordable price point by using an A18 Pro chip, which is used in the iPhone 16 Pro.

items.title: The new MacBook Pro laptops are as much as $400 more expensive than their predecessors. Thank the RAM shortage.
items.content: With the demands for more computers and data centers to power AI, the market is experiencing a shortage of RAM, causing memory prices to surge.

items.title: The billion-dollar infrastructure deals powering the AI boom
items.content: Here's everything we know about the biggest AI infrastructure projects, including major spending from Meta, Oracle, Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI.

items.title: The White House wants AI companies to cover rate hikes. Most have already said they would.
items.content: Many hyperscalers have already made public commitments to cover electricity cost increases.

items.title: The public opposition to AI infrastructure is heating up
items.content: Public backlash over the data center boom is leading to a variety of draconian policies — including bans on new construction.

items.title: All the important news from the ongoing India AI Impact Summit
items.content: India is hosting a four-day AI Summit this week that will be attended by executives from major AI labs and Big Tech, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Nvidia, Microsoft, Google, an

items.title: Live Nation, Ticketmaster’s Owner, Settles Antitrust Case With Justice Dept.
items.content: The agreement, which requires a judicial sign-off, would avoid a breakup of the concert giant. But states who joined the suit object to the terms.

items.title: Countries in Asia Try to Contain the Economic Fallout of the Mideast Conflict
items.content: Across Asia, where countries are highly exposed to rising oil and gas costs and tightening supply, governments are acting to mitigate economic harm.

items.title: Investors and Trump Face an Iran Dilemma
items.content: A spike in oil prices has roiled stocks and bonds again and threatens to accelerate inflation — adding pressure on the White House.

items.title: ‘The Old Game Is Back’: Oil Is a Potent Geopolitical Tool Again
items.content: The war in the Middle East has sent prices soaring, showing how much the world continues to depend on reliable supplies of oil and gas.
